title: In 1989, the Soviet Union had a trade deal with Pepsi. Because the ruble had no international value at the time, the USSR paid Pepsi in warships, including 17 subs, briefly making Pepsi the world's 6th largest navy.
why_it_matters: The Pepsi-Soviet warship deal is one of history's most surreal corporate footnotes — resurfacing now with new resonance as global trade fragmentation and currency instability are live concerns again.
